description:
If /etc/at.deny exists, it must be owned by root. To properly set the owner of /etc/at.deny, run the command: 
$ sudo chown root /etc/at.deny
rationale:
If the owner of the at.deny file is not set to root, the possibility exists for an
unauthorized user to view or edit sensitive information.
ocil_clause: /etc/at.deny does not have an owner of root
ocil:
To check the ownership of /etc/at.deny, run the command: 
$ ls -lL /etc/at.deny
If properly configured, the output should indicate the following owner: root
title: Verify User Who Owns /etc/at.deny file
